title: Jeannie's Visit

jokes.text: My next guest, I'm sure you'll recognize. She is an inter... I can't even say this... she's an internationally known model and actress.
jokes.setup: Larry introduces his guest with confidence
jokes.punchline: He stumbles over 'internationally' revealing his nervousness/incompetence
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment - Larry's professional facade cracking under pressure. The stammering feels authentic to his neurotic persona.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: My next guest, I'm sure you'll recognize. She is an inter... I can't even say this... she's an internationally known model and actress.

jokes.text: I'm American, and I'm used to only kissing on one cheek. That's OK. Second of all, just being who I am, I'm always just happy with one kiss
jokes.setup: Larry explains his awkwardness with European greeting customs
jokes.punchline: The self-deprecating admission that he's 'always just happy with one kiss'
jokes.explanation: Excellent character work - Larry's sexual insecurity masked as cultural observation. The line works on multiple levels.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: I'm American, and I'm used to only kissing on one cheek. That's OK. Second of all, just being who I am, I'm always just happy with one kiss

jokes.text: I figure the second one's... I'm gonna somehow get hurt. [...] Usually a kiss on one side and a hit on the other.
jokes.setup: Larry's paranoia about the second cheek kiss
jokes.punchline: His expectation that affection will be followed by violence
jokes.explanation: Dark, psychologically revealing humor that's quintessentially Larry Sanders - neurotic paranoia dressed as social commentary.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: I figure the second one's... I'm gonna somehow get hurt. Usually a kiss on one side and a hit on the other.

jokes.text: If you were asian, we would have bowed.
jokes.setup: Larry trying to be culturally sophisticated about greetings
jokes.punchline: The casual stereotyping that hangs in the air awkwardly
jokes.explanation: Dated cultural humor that lands more as cringe than laugh, but fits Larry's oblivious character.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: If you were asian, we would have bowed.

jokes.text: I love oslo. That's in norway. Yes, it's norway.
jokes.setup: Larry tries to show cultural knowledge about Sweden
jokes.punchline: He confidently mentions Oslo, which is in Norway, not Sweden
jokes.explanation: Classic Larry moment - his desperation to appear worldly backfires spectacularly with basic geography fail.

dialogue.line: I love oslo.
That's in norway.
Yes, it's norway.

jokes.text: Paula! Paula, you gotta help me. Jeannie's coming up in the elevator.
jokes.setup: Someone urgently calling for help
jokes.punchline: The 'emergency' is Larry's ex-wife arriving unexpectedly
jokes.explanation: Good setup for the episode's main conflict, treating a personal situation like a professional crisis.

dialogue.line: Paula! Paula, you gotta help me. Jeannie's coming up in the elevator.

jokes.text: Wow, that is so rare to find someone else who likes Italian food.
jokes.setup: Tatjana says she likes Italian food
jokes.punchline: Larry acts like this is incredibly unique when Italian food is universally popular
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan delivery of obvious sarcasm. Larry's desperate attempt to find connection over the most common preference possible.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: Wow, that is so rare to find someone else who likes Italian food.

jokes.text: No, I'm asking the guy with one eye in row 6. I don't think he knows either.
jokes.setup: Someone asks Artie a question he can't answer
jokes.punchline: Artie sarcastically redirects to a random audience member, adding the visual detail of 'one eye'
jokes.explanation: Classic Artie - cynical, specific, and cutting. The random physical detail makes it memorable.

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: No, I'm asking the guy with one eye in row 6. I don't think he knows either.

jokes.text: Don't get drunk.
jokes.setup: Artie leaving someone in charge of the show
jokes.punchline: His only instruction is not to get drunk - implying low expectations
jokes.explanation: Perfect Artie line - economical, cynical, and revealing about the show's standards.

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: Don't get drunk.

jokes.text: Again? We have David duchovny again.
jokes.setup: Larry announces tomorrow's guests
jokes.punchline: He breaks character to complain about booking the same guest repeatedly
jokes.explanation: Meta moment where Larry drops the professional facade, revealing the mundane reality behind the show.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: Again? We have David duchovny again.

jokes.text: Larry's office hasn't changed a bit. Oh, is, uh, that right?
jokes.setup: Jeannie makes casual observation about Larry's office
jokes.punchline: Larry's stilted, uncomfortable response reveals the tension
jokes.explanation: Subtle character work showing Larry's discomfort through awkward formality with his ex-wife.

dialogue.speaker: Jeannie
dialogue.line: Larry's office hasn't changed a bit.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: Oh, is, uh, that right?

jokes.text: I don't watch TV.
jokes.setup: Someone explains who Dana Delany is
jokes.punchline: The ex-wife of a TV show host doesn't watch TV
jokes.explanation: Economical character reveal - Jeannie's detachment from Larry's world, which probably contributed to their divorce.

dialogue.line: I don't watch TV.

jokes.text: The wayans family is in there. A lot of fun going on. Hey, big house party.
jokes.setup: Artie suggesting Jeannie go to the green room
jokes.punchline: His overselling of what's clearly just people waiting around
jokes.explanation: Artie trying to manage an awkward situation with transparent enthusiasm.

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: The wayans family is in there. A lot of fun going on. Hey, big house party.

jokes.text: Mucho blood, but, you know, not as bad as it looks. Lot of veins in the scalp.
jokes.setup: Someone asks about the juggler who hit his head
jokes.punchline: Larry's matter-of-fact medical assessment of head trauma
jokes.explanation: Dark humor callback with unexpectedly clinical detail. Larry treating serious injury as show business trivia.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: Mucho blood, but, you know, not as bad as it looks. Lot of veins in the scalp.

jokes.text: I just have dinner every night with the third guest. It just happens to be tatjana tonight.
jokes.setup: Larry explaining why he can't have dinner with Jeannie
jokes.punchline: His transparently made-up 'rule' about always dining with the third guest
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry moment - obviously lying while trying to sound professional and systematic.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: I just have dinner every night with the third guest. It just happens to be tatjana tonight.

jokes.text: Vendela was right. It's so terrific in here.
jokes.setup: Tatjana compliments Larry's office
jokes.punchline: She references another supermodel who apparently also visited Larry's office
jokes.explanation: Subtle implication of Larry's pattern with supermodels, delivered innocuously by Tatjana.

dialogue.speaker: Tatjana
dialogue.line: Vendela was right. It's so terrific in here.

jokes.text: I can't believe I drove an hour for that.
jokes.setup: Larry's awkward interaction with Jeannie
jokes.punchline: Tatjana's blunt assessment of the underwhelming encounter
jokes.explanation: Tatjana's matter-of-fact disappointment cuts through the tension with European directness.

dialogue.speaker: Tatjana
dialogue.line: I can't believe I drove an hour for that.

jokes.text: You know these supermodels have no stored fat. When they gotta eat, they gotta eat.
jokes.setup: Explaining why Tatjana left for dinner
jokes.punchline: Hank's pseudo-scientific explanation treating supermodels like wild animals
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd Hank logic, delivered with complete confidence. The biological explanation for social awkwardness is perfect character comedy.

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: You know these supermodels have no stored fat. When they gotta eat, they gotta eat.

jokes.text: It's so typical. You know, I warm 'em up and just... Larry scores.
jokes.setup: Hank discussing Larry going to dinner with Tatjana
jokes.punchline: Hank's delusional belief that he 'warms up' women for Larry
jokes.explanation: Perfect Hank delusion - taking credit for something that never happened while missing what actually did happen (with Jeannie).

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: It's so typical. You know, I warm 'em up and just... Larry scores.

jokes.text: He looked right through me. Oh, I know. I know. It's a cold stare.
jokes.setup: Jeannie describing Larry's reaction to her
jokes.punchline: Hank immediately agreeing and adding his own interpretation
jokes.explanation: Hank's eagerness to bond over mutual resentment of Larry, setting up their connection.

dialogue.speaker: Jeannie
dialogue.line: He looked right through me.

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: Oh, I know. I know. It's a cold stare.

jokes.text: Yeah, like, um, hi. I'm a star. Who are you?
jokes.setup: Describing Larry's attitude
jokes.punchline: Jeannie's spot-on impression of Larry's arrogance
jokes.explanation: Effective character assassination through mimicry, capturing Larry's celebrity entitlement.

dialogue.speaker: Jeannie
dialogue.line: Yeah, like, um, hi. I'm a star. Who are you?

jokes.text: I also do cagney, you know.
jokes.setup: After doing an impression of Larry
jokes.punchline: Hank randomly mentions his James Cagney impression
jokes.explanation: Classic Hank - turning a moment of connection into self-promotion with random tangent.

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: I also do cagney, you know.

jokes.text: His family completely screwed him up. [...] did they spank him? Yes. Constantly.
jokes.setup: Hank asking about Larry's childhood
jokes.punchline: The immediate, matter-of-fact confirmation of childhood trauma
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about childhood trauma delivered casually, showing how ex-spouses share intimate damage.

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: His family completely screwed him up. [...] did they spank him?

dialogue.speaker: Jeannie
dialogue.line: Yes. Constantly.

jokes.text: You dirty rat. You killed my brother, see? And now I'm gonna kill you, see?
jokes.setup: Hank mentioned doing Cagney impressions
jokes.punchline: He delivers a terrible, clichéd Cagney impression
jokes.explanation: Predictable bad impression that's funny because it's so exactly what you'd expect from Hank - hackneyed and obvious.

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: You dirty rat. You killed my brother, see? And now I'm gonna kill you, see?

jokes.text: Hank, that's my dress.
jokes.setup: Jeannie getting dressed to leave
jokes.punchline: Hank is apparently wearing her dress
jokes.explanation: Perfect visual gag that tells the whole story of their night in four words. Economical and shocking.

dialogue.speaker: Jeannie
dialogue.line: Hank, that's my dress.

jokes.text: I ate everything in the mini bar while you were asleep. I'll send you a check.
jokes.setup: Hank's awkward morning-after confession
jokes.punchline: His pathetic admission of eating everything while she slept
jokes.explanation: Perfect Hank moment - making bad situations worse with pathetic honesty and misguided politeness.

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: I ate everything in the mini bar while you were asleep. I'll send you a check.

jokes.text: Mr. Washington, you know, these wooden teeth will last a lifetime, as long as you don't put a, uh, Beaver in your mouth.
jokes.setup: Hank playing George Washington in a sketch
jokes.punchline: Terrible pun about beavers and wooden teeth
jokes.explanation: Terrible pun that's supposed to be terrible - fits Hank's character as someone who'd think this is clever.

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: Mr. Washington, you know, these wooden teeth will last a lifetime, as long as you don't put a, uh, Beaver in your mouth.

jokes.text: There's too many lines, or are the words just too big?
jokes.setup: Hank struggling with his lines
jokes.punchline: His innocent question about whether the words are too big
jokes.explanation: Perfect Hank moment - his genuine confusion about his own intellectual limitations, delivered without irony.

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: There's too many lines, or are the words just too big?

jokes.text: Well, before or after I fucked her?
jokes.setup: Larry asking if Jeannie seemed happy
jokes.punchline: Hank's crude revelation about sleeping with her
jokes.explanation: Perfect setup and devastating punchline. Hank's casual crudeness combined with the bombshell revelation creates maximum impact.

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: Well, before or after I fucked her?

jokes.text: I know it wasn't during 'cause you would have been nauseous.
jokes.setup: After revealing he slept with Jeannie
jokes.punchline: Hank's self-aware joke about his own unattractiveness
jokes.explanation: Hank doubling down with self-deprecating humor, showing awareness of his own ridiculousness.

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: I know it wasn't during 'cause you would have been nauseous.

jokes.text: What do you mean, she slipped on something and fell onto your penis?
jokes.setup: Hank saying it was an 'accident'
jokes.punchline: Larry's sarcastic deconstruction of the 'accident' excuse
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist logic applied to a ridiculous excuse. Highly quotable and perfectly captures Larry's sardonic intelligence.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: What do you mean, she slipped on something and fell onto your penis?

jokes.text: I can't believe that Hank drilled Jeannie.
jokes.setup: Phil overhearing the conversation
jokes.punchline: His crude summary using workplace slang
jokes.explanation: Crude but effective - shows how the revelation spreads and becomes workplace gossip.

dialogue.speaker: Phil
dialogue.line: I can't believe that Hank drilled Jeannie.

jokes.text: Sweetheart, baby, trying to ruin us all? Our jobs depend on Larry being just that much better than the other guy.
jokes.setup: Artie confronting Hank about sleeping with Jeannie
jokes.punchline: The cynical reality that their livelihoods depend on Larry's fragile ego
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-commentary on show business and celebrity psychology. Artie's protective cynicism perfectly captures the stakes.

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: Sweetheart, baby, trying to ruin us all? Our jobs depend on Larry being just that much better than the other guy.

jokes.text: If he feels so bad, why doesn't he pay for the cappuccino runs? He's got the money.
jokes.setup: Beverly explaining Larry bought a cappuccino machine
jokes.punchline: Her immediate cynical read of Larry's 'generous' gesture
jokes.explanation: Perfect Beverly - immediately cutting through false generosity to reveal the cheap reality underneath.

dialogue.speaker: Beverly
dialogue.line: If he feels so bad, why doesn't he pay for the cappuccino runs? He's got the money.

jokes.text: Because it's an empty gesture made in the spirit of cheapness.
jokes.setup: Asked why she's being ungrateful
jokes.punchline: Beverly's perfectly articulated cynicism
jokes.explanation: Perfectly crafted line that encapsulates Beverly's character and Larry's boss psychology. The phrase 'spirit of cheapness' is brilliant.

dialogue.speaker: Beverly
dialogue.line: Because it's an empty gesture made in the spirit of cheapness.

jokes.text: This is a beautiful plate, but not as beautiful as the plate in your head.
jokes.setup: Hank commenting on a fan gift
jokes.punchline: His nonsensical attempt at a compliment
jokes.explanation: Classic Hank - trying to be poetic and profound but creating meaningless word salad.

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: This is a beautiful plate, but not as beautiful as the plate in your head.

jokes.text: It's nice the inmates get time for arts and crafts.
jokes.setup: Looking at a fan-made gift
jokes.punchline: Larry's assumption it was made in prison
jokes.explanation: Dark humor revealing Larry's cynicism about his fanbase. Economical and cutting.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: It's nice the inmates get time for arts and crafts.

jokes.text: You're a talentless fat fuck, you know that? Wow. I'm sorry? I'm sorry. Did I say... talentless fat fuck, I meant... I meant, uh, sidekick.
jokes.setup: Larry's rage at Hank building up
jokes.punchline: His 'correction' that equates sidekick with talentless fat fuck
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation where Larry's 'apology' is actually more insulting. Perfect character moment showing his cruelty.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: You're a talentless fat fuck, you know that?

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: Wow. I'm sorry? I'm sorry. Did I say... talentless fat fuck, I meant... I meant, uh, sidekick.

jokes.text: So, well, Jeannie tells me you wet your little bed till you were 8?
jokes.setup: After Larry's insult
jokes.punchline: Hank retaliating with embarrassing intimate information
jokes.explanation: Effective escalation using pillow talk against Larry, though the humor is more cringe than clever.

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: So, well, Jeannie tells me you wet your little bed till you were 8?

jokes.text: Oh, and fell onto his penis? Just as I suspected.
jokes.setup: Jeannie using Larry's exact phrase back at him
jokes.punchline: Her confirmation that she heard their earlier conversation
jokes.explanation: Effective callback showing Jeannie's intelligence and that she overheard Larry's crude joke.

dialogue.speaker: Jeannie
dialogue.line: Oh, and fell onto his penis? Just as I suspected.

jokes.text: Oh, God! Larry doesn't like white water rafting! Oh, God! Larry doesn't like white water rafting!
jokes.setup: Larry's anger about Jeannie sharing private information
jokes.punchline: His absurd impression of what she might have yelled during sex
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd escalation where Larry imagines the most ridiculous possible pillow talk scenario.

dialogue.speaker: Jeannie
dialogue.line: Oh, God! Larry doesn't like white water rafting! Oh, God! Larry doesn't like white water rafting!

jokes.text: Thanks for a purr-fect evening? [...] Then it can't be too serious 'cause otherwise, you know, he would've drawn a pony. That's what he's best at.
jokes.setup: Examining Hank's drawing of a kitty cat
jokes.punchline: Larry's specific knowledge of Hank's artistic limitations
jokes.explanation: Perfect character detail - Larry's intimate knowledge of Hank's pathetic artistic abilities and their hierarchy.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: Thanks for a purr-fect evening? [...] Then it can't be too serious 'cause otherwise, you know, he would've drawn a pony. That's what he's best at.

jokes.text: The only reason that I slept with Hank was to hurt you. But... but I don't want you to tell him that, 'cause I don't want to hurt his feelings.
jokes.setup: Jeannie's confession about her motivation
jokes.punchline: Her concern for Hank's feelings despite using him as a weapon
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character complexity - cruel manipulation combined with genuine kindness toward the victim.

dialogue.speaker: Jeannie
dialogue.line: The only reason that I slept with Hank was to hurt you. But... but I don't want you to tell him that, 'cause I don't want to hurt his feelings.

jokes.text: Wow, did you read that off of a greeting card? That was sweet.
jokes.setup: Hank's overly sentimental response
jokes.punchline: Larry's cutting dismissal of Hank's attempt at depth
jokes.explanation: Sharp put-down that perfectly captures Larry's disdain for Hank's emotional intelligence.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: Wow, did you read that off of a greeting card? That was sweet.

jokes.text: The sun sets at night so it can be at home to watch your monologue.
jokes.setup: Hank's rant about Larry's ego
jokes.punchline: The absurdly poetic image of the sun organizing its schedule around Larry
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd metaphor that captures both Hank's poetic pretensions and his legitimate grievance.

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: The sun sets at night so it can be at home to watch your monologue.

jokes.text: There they are, the sunshine boys.
jokes.setup: Larry and Hank covered in cappuccino after their fight
jokes.punchline: Artie's sarcastic reference to the Neil Simon play about aging comedians
jokes.explanation: Smart cultural reference that works on multiple levels - the visual state they're in and their contentious relationship.

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: There they are, the sunshine boys.

jokes.text: Researchers have found that scuba diving may cause brain damage. Well, there's a shock to the people who watch baywatch.
jokes.setup: News story about scuba diving and brain damage
jokes.punchline: Connecting it to Baywatch viewers as already brain damaged
jokes.explanation: Standard topical humor from Larry's monologue. Competent but not particularly original.

dialogue.line: Researchers have found that scuba diving may cause brain damage. Well, there's a shock to the people who watch baywatch.

jokes.text: Now there's where you just got to say, 'hey, son, you're grounded.'
jokes.setup: News about a 12-year-old possibly involved in Oklahoma bombing
jokes.punchline: Treating domestic terrorism as a typical parenting issue
jokes.explanation: Dark topical humor that works by understating the severity. Decent monologue material but feels dated.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: Now there's where you just got to say, 'hey, son, you're grounded.'

jokes.text: Whenever I come to L.A. I get all confused and... and twisted around. L... I hate L.A.
jokes.setup: Jeannie explaining her behavior
jokes.punchline: Her simple, honest assessment that L.A. makes her crazy
jokes.explanation: Relatable character moment that explains her out-of-character behavior. Universal L.A. experience.

dialogue.speaker: Jeannie
dialogue.line: Whenever I come to L.A. I get all confused and... and twisted around. L... I hate L.A.

jokes.text: You're marrying someone named stu?
jokes.setup: Jeannie announces her engagement
jokes.punchline: Larry's focus on the unglamorous name rather than the engagement itself
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry moment - his shallow focus on superficial details when faced with emotional news.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: You're marrying someone named stu?

jokes.text: I hope you don't take this the wrong way, but you're never coming back again, are you? No.
jokes.setup: Artie's seemingly polite farewell
jokes.punchline: His blunt question about her permanent departure and her honest answer
jokes.explanation: Perfect Artie moment - cutting through politeness to get practical information. Both characters' honesty makes it work.

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: I hope you don't take this the wrong way, but you're never coming back again, are you?

dialogue.speaker: Jeannie
dialogue.line: No.

tmdb_overview: A surprise visit by Larry's ex-wife causes an unusual triangle between her, Larry and Hank. Larry offers a cappuccino machine as a substitute for the daily office run to Starbucks.
